lens: NEWCOMER ONBOARDING — speed and unambiguity of the path from landing to first successful run(), then to the second task; whether there is exactly ONE obvious path with no competing front doors.

scores.rationale: Tutorial-first (FastAPI model) is the strongest design for THIS lens specifically. The newcomer path is a literal rail: single 'Start Here' card -> Get Started (pip install + ONE inline run() on generate.regression() synthetic data, succeeds with zero files in <5 min) -> the page's ONLY forward link is 'Continue to the Tutorial' -> Lesson 1 swaps the synthetic dataset for the user's own folder, Lesson 2 adds exactly ONE concept (SNV) with a persistent prev/next rail. The SECOND task is unambiguous and built-in: it is literally 'Next ->'. This is the only candidate where the second task is structurally pre-decided rather than chosen from a menu, the single highest-value property for onboarding. It directly dissolves audit #6 (the 6-card grid I confirmed in index.md: Getting Started/Concepts/User Guide/Reference/Developer/Architecture Deep Dive with no canonical start) by collapsing 5 doors to 1 and DEMOTING concepts/ and onboarding/ to appendices reached only via 'Why it works ->' side-links, so they cannot re-compete as front doors. Every snippet is doctest/literalinclude-executed per-PR, so the copy-paste-fatal class (result.predict() #3, bundle= #4, the current Quickstart's stale sample_data/regression path I confirmed) becomes a build failure on the exact on-ramp where the audit says newcomers are misled within 10 minutes. Not a 10 only because the strictly linear spine makes 'I already have a specific task' detours depend on a well-indexed Recipes appendix the design admits is secondary; a goal-carrying NIRS newcomer must still walk the rail or trust appendix indexing.

Task/Persona-first is nearly as strong and arguably better for the realistic NIRS newcomer who lands with a goal ('I have a regression dataset', 'I need to pick preprocessing') rather than a learning goal. Path: single 'Start here' card (no 6-way grid) -> 60-second synthetic run() on generate.regression(), first run achieved on screen one -> 'Choose your task' router into one of 7 goal tracks, each walking data -> inline pipeline -> run() -> result -> predict() to a shipped artifact. It collapses the 4 competing front doors to 1 and REALIZES persona_paths.md's intent correctly (the 7 tracks ARE the persona paths) while DELETING its 5 broken refs and fake load_dataset() (#7/#8) instead of patching. The SECOND task is well-served by bottom-of-track 'Next goals' links (Regression -> Preprocessing selection / CV) so the user moves goal->goal without returning to a menu. Two onboarding risks keep it below candidate 1: (a) the router is a choice point — a true novice who does not yet know whether their problem is 'regression' vs 'preprocessing selection' faces a 7-way fork right after the first run, more cognitive load than candidate 1's single 'Next'; (b) 7 top-level tracks is more surface than one spine, and a thin track (Transfer/retrain) risks a dead-endy feel for an early wanderer. Mitigated by mode-badging and thin-track-as-single-page, but the fork is real.

Strict Diataxis is the most principled and most maintainable, and it DOES fix the central structural defect (#6) cleanly: index.md becomes a thin router with one 'Start here -> Quickstart' CTA, each of the 4 competing front doors maps to exactly one quadrant, and the path to first run() is a single click to a doctest-executed Quickstart. For 'landing -> first run()' it is essentially tied with the others. Where it is weaker ON THIS LENS is the SECOND task and the 'one obvious path' feel beyond the Quickstart. The landing presents FIVE secondary cards (Tutorials/How-to/Reference/Explanation + Examples) the instant the user looks past the CTA — closer to the current confusing 6-card grid I confirmed than to FastAPI's single rail; four-quadrant nav is exactly the equal-weight presentation that makes a novice hesitate. Worse for onboarding, strict purity FRAGMENTS a single topic across four pages (a 'branching' learner is sent to Tutorial + How-to + Explanation + Reference), which the candidate lists as its own #1 con; a newcomer wants the topic in one place. The Quickstart's single 'Next' does hand off to the Tutorial (good), so the path exists, but the IA optimizes for long-term findability and contributor governance, not the novice's shortest unambiguous arc.

best_ideas_to_graft: The single forward link with NO competing choice (candidate 1): the Get Started page's only outbound link is 'Continue to the Tutorial', and each Tutorial lesson's only primary action is 'Next ->'. This is the strongest onboarding mechanic of any candidate — it makes the second task structurally pre-decided instead of a menu choice. Graft this 'one primary CTA per on-ramp page' rule onto whichever IA wins, including the task-first router.

Lesson 1 = swap the synthetic dataset for the user's OWN folder path, still one inline run() (candidate 1). The progression 'succeed on synthetic in 5 min, then change exactly one thing (your data)' is the lowest-friction bridge from the zero-data first run to a real first run. Both other candidates jump straight to the user's CSV; this intermediate step measurably de-risks the second contact.

Realize persona_paths.md as real navigation, not a content page (candidate 2 does it best, candidate 0 converges): the 7 goal tracks / 'learning paths' links ARE the persona routing, done correctly, which lets persona_paths.md be DELETED with its 5 broken refs and fake load_dataset() (#7/#8) rather than patched. Even a tutorial-first IA should put a small 'I already have a goal ->' router block on the Get Started page so the goal-carrying NIRS newcomer is not forced down the linear spine.

The zero-data first run via generate.regression() as THE canonical first snippet (all three; already present but buried as a secondary 'Try Without Data' tip in the current index.md). It removes the #1 onboarding blocker — needing a dataset before you can succeed — and is doctest-runnable in CI. Make it the literal first copy-paste on the single Start-here page, not a secondary tip.

Per-page Diataxis-mode badge while navigating by task/lesson (candidate 2's 'badge each track with its Diataxis mode'). This grafts candidate 0's theoretical clarity (the reader always knows whether they are learning / looking-up / understanding) onto candidate 1/2's friendlier non-Diataxis top-level nav, getting the orientation benefit without the four-quadrant fragmentation that hurts novices.

Bottom-of-page 'Next goals ->' links that move goal->goal and never return the user to a front-door menu (candidate 2). Combined with candidate 1's single-Next rail, this guarantees the user never re-encounters the ambiguous landing grid after they have started.

Cross-link sideways from every learning step to its ONE Explanation page ('Why it works ->') and its runnable gallery script ('Run this yourself ->') (candidates 0 and 1). This keeps the on-ramp linear and uncluttered while making the 'why' reachable in one click, so concepts/ never competes as a front door (audit #6) yet is never more than one hop away.
verdict: Through the newcomer-onboarding lens, candidate 1 (Tutorial-first / FastAPI) wins because it is the only architecture where BOTH the first run() AND the second task are structurally pre-decided: a single 'Start Here' card -> a zero-data inline run() that succeeds in under 5 minutes -> a single 'Continue to the Tutorial' link -> a strictly linear lesson rail whose second task is literally 'Next ->'. There is exactly one path and no point on it offers a competing 'also start here' choice — the precise opposite of the confirmed current state (a 6-card landing grid in index.md, a Quickstart that teaches the anti-pattern and points at a non-existent sample_data/regression path, and 4-5 competing front doors per audit #6). Candidate 2 (task/persona-first) is a very close second and is arguably superior for the goal-carrying NIRS practitioner, but its post-first-run 'choose your task' router reintroduces a 7-way fork that adds cognitive load for a true novice who does not yet know which goal bucket they are in. Candidate 0 (strict Diataxis) is the most maintainable and cleanly kills the competing-front-doors defect, but for raw onboarding it is third: its four equal mode cards echo the current multi-card grid, and its purity fragments any single topic across four cross-linked pages — the opposite of what a newcomer wants. The decisive grafts are candidate 1's 'one primary CTA per on-ramp page' and 'Lesson 1 = swap synthetic data for your own folder' mechanics, candidate 2's correct realization of persona routing as the goal-router (deleting persona_paths.md and its broken refs), and the universally-shared generate.regression() zero-data first run as the literal first copy-paste. The ideal onboarding IA is candidate 1's single linear rail with a small candidate-2 'I already have a goal ->' router grafted onto the Start-here page and candidate-0's mode badges for orientation.

lens: Maintainability & single-source-of-truth: does the architecture eliminate the competing front doors and the api/↔reference/ duplication, document each concept in exactly one canonical place, and minimize the ongoing drift the structure permits?

scores.rationale: Strongest SSOT by construction, not by convention. Its load-bearing maintainability invariant — 'Explanation owns concepts, Reference owns parameters, neither restates the other; they link' — is structural: a parameter list lives in exactly one file (reference/), a concept's 'why' in exactly one (concepts/Explanation), so the api/↔reference and concept-vs-concept duplication the audit documents (#18, #6, #38) cannot recur. Critically it is the ONLY candidate with a mechanical placement rule a contributor can apply without topic judgment: 'which of 4 modes is the reader's posture?' — that is what makes the rule durable governance rather than a one-off cleanup. Front-door dissolution is concrete and verified against the real tree: getting_started/concepts.md DELETED, persona_paths.md DELETED (killing its #7 load_dataset and #8 broken refs with it, not patching them), onboarding/ fully dissolved into concepts/ — i.e. it removes the duplicating files, it does not merely re-link them. Reference unification is per-page and matches what I confirmed on disk: api/storage.md (794 LOC) merges into reference/storage.md (404), api/workspace.md (125) into reference/workspace.md (208), the 357 autodoc stubs relocate UNDER reference/api/ (one tree, not parallel), generator triplication (#38) collapses to one canonical page the others link to, session.md orphan (#26) wired in. Honest residual-drift channel, correctly owned in its cons: 'one page = one mode' purity is human-policed (CI lints style/metadata/refs but not mode-purity), so concept-bleed from Tutorial/How-to back into Explanation is the one place drift can re-enter — this is why it is 9 not 10. Loses no point for fragmentation per se (a maintainability non-issue if cross-links hold) but does for that un-mechanizable purity check.

Identical, equally strong Reference-SSOT and metadata-SSOT to C1 (api/ dissolves INTO reference/ — same per-page merges, same autosummary-under-reference, same generator-collapse, same source-derived metadata), and an identical CI safety-net, so on the mechanical-drift axis it ties C1. It loses on NARRATIVE SSOT by deliberate design: concepts are taught in the Tutorial spine AND explained in a thin Concepts appendix — its own words, 'TAUGHT in one Tutorial lesson, EXPLAINED in one Concepts page.' That is two homes for one concept. To teach branching, Lesson N must restate the branch/merge idea; concepts/branching then explains it again. This is the api/↔concepts duplication pattern reborn one layer up, and the boundary between 'teaching prose' and 'explanation prose' is editorial, not structural — exactly the kind of soft seam where the audit's drift lives. Mitigations (one-new-concept-per-lesson, 'Why it works ->' cross-links) reduce but do not eliminate it, because the cross-link does not stop the lesson from re-stating the concept. It also DEMOTES concepts/ and onboarding/ to appendices rather than deleting the overlap, and its own con flags 'real risk of dropping good prose if rushed.' Single front door is achieved cleanly (5->1). Net: front-door defect solved, Reference duplication solved, but a new, structurally-permitted Tutorial-vs-Concepts duplication is introduced, so ongoing narrative drift is materially higher than C1.

Best generated-artifact SSOT of the three: autosummary-recursive over the 357 symbols means new public symbols appear with zero manual edits, and a gallery generated from disk means counts/U05/U06/R04 'self-heal' — those classes of drift become structurally impossible, which is genuinely excellent. Reference unification (api/ eliminated into reference/, the 4 hand-written pages merged/deleted) matches C1/C2. But on the CORE of this lens — each concept in exactly one canonical narrative place, minimal permitted drift — it is the weakest, and its own #1 con is the confession: 'content is legitimately cross-cutting (a preprocessing step used in Regression AND Classification): tracks must link the canonical page, never re-explain — requires authoring discipline.' Seven task tracks each walk the SAME data->pipeline->run->predict->export arc, so the same workflow steps (loading, preprocessing, CV, export) are structurally INVITED into multiple tracks, and there is no mechanical placement rule (unlike C1's posture test) that forbids a preprocessing explanation living in both the Regression track and the Preprocessing-selection track. This re-creates the audit's exact 3-4x concept duplication (#6), merely re-keyed from 'front door' to 'goal.' It correctly realizes persona_paths' intent as the tracks while deleting its broken refs (good), and meets the user at the goal (best findability), but findability is not this lens. The thin shared Concepts layer is the right instinct, yet 'tracks must never re-explain' is a convention reviewers must enforce per-PR, not an invariant the structure guarantees — so permitted ongoing drift is the highest of the three.

best_ideas_to_graft: From C1: the structural SSOT invariant stated as a hard rule — 'Explanation owns the why, Reference owns the signature/parameters, neither restates the other; the only permitted relationship between them is a hyperlink.' This is the single most drift-proof sentence across all three; adopt it verbatim as the governing maintainability law regardless of which top-level skeleton wins.

From C1: the mechanical, topic-agnostic placement rule ('which mode is the reader's posture?'). Any winning IA needs a deterministic answer to 'where does a NEW page go?' that a contributor can apply without judgment. C2's spine and C3's tasks both lack this; graft C1's decision procedure onto whichever structure is chosen, as a contributor checklist in CONTRIBUTING.

From C3: generate Reference AND the example gallery FROM SOURCE/DISK (autosummary-recursive for the 357 symbols; sphinx-gallery/literalinclude over examples/*.py). Generated-not-handwritten is the only true zero-drift mechanism in the whole exercise — counts, U05/U06, R04, and new public symbols self-heal. This belongs in every candidate and is the highest-leverage anti-drift idea present.

From all three (converged): the api/ tree is DELETED, not maintained beside reference/ — the 4 hand-written pages merge into their reference/ twins (verified on disk: storage 794->404, workspace 125->208), the 357 stubs mount UNDER reference/api/ as one tree, generator syntax (#38) collapses to ONE canonical page the others link to. Make 'two trees documenting one surface is structurally impossible afterward' an explicit acceptance criterion.

From all three: CI gates that convert the 'ships silently' defect class to build failures — doc-snippet execution (doctest/myst-nb), per-PR fast example subset (replacing weekly cron), tightened nitpick (remove the blanket nirs4all.* nitpick_ignore_regex), and metadata-from-source with a license-string grep gate that checks the string matches LICENSE WITHOUT banning the legitimate CeCILL token. This is the safety-net floor; without it ANY architecture re-rots to 648->700.

From C1 (and a hedge against C2/C3's weakness): DELETE the duplicating front-door pages outright (getting_started/concepts, persona_paths, onboarding/) rather than re-linking them. Deletion eliminates a drift source permanently; re-linking (C2's 'demote to appendix', C3's 'link the canonical page') leaves the second copy alive and therefore able to drift.

From C3: realize persona/role routing as NAVIGATION (links/cards), never as duplicated CONTENT. C1 makes the same move (persona_paths value re-expressed as learning-path links). The grafted principle: routing aids may point at the one canonical page many ways, but must never restate it — this is the discipline that keeps C3's task tracks (if chosen) from re-duplicating concepts.
verdict: On the maintainability and single-source-of-truth lens specifically, Candidate 1 (Strict Diátaxis) wins clearly and should anchor the architecture. It is the only candidate that makes per-concept SSOT a STRUCTURAL invariant rather than an editorial convention: the four-mode split with the rule 'Explanation owns concepts, Reference owns parameters, neither restates the other — they only link' means the api/↔reference duplication (#18) and the 3-4x concept duplication (#6) cannot structurally recur, and it is the only candidate offering a mechanical, topic-agnostic placement rule ('which mode is the reader's posture?') that tells a future contributor where a new page goes without judgment — which is what converts a one-time cleanup into durable governance. It also DELETES the duplicating front-door pages (getting_started/concepts, persona_paths, onboarding) rather than re-linking them, removing drift sources permanently. Its one honest residual risk — 'one page = one mode' purity is human-policed because CI cannot lint mode-purity — is the only reason it is 9 not 10. Candidate 2 ties C1 on Reference and metadata SSOT and on the CI safety-net, but its tutorial-first design structurally permits a NEW duplication (concept TAUGHT in the spine AND EXPLAINED in the Concepts appendix), so it carries higher ongoing narrative drift; it is a strong second. Candidate 3 has the best generated-from-source anti-drift mechanics (graft these) and best domain findability, but on THIS lens it is weakest: its seven task tracks each re-walk the same workflow arc with no mechanical rule forbidding a concept from living in multiple tracks, which its own #1 con concedes re-creates the audit's exact concept-duplication problem keyed by goal. Recommended synthesis: adopt C1's four-mode SSOT skeleton and placement rule as the spine, graft C3's generate-Reference-and-gallery-from-source as the zero-drift implementation of the Reference and Examples estates, and keep the shared CI safety-net all three propose as the non-negotiable floor.

lens: FIT TO nirs4all's ACTUAL SURFACE — does the architecture match the real public API (run/predict/explain/retrain/session/generate + RunResult/PredictResult/ExplainResult), the real pipeline/generator/branching feature set, and the examples-as-tests reality (U/D/R prefixes, R04 missing, weekly CI), without inventing structure the library does not support?

scores.rationale: Strict-Diataxis. Strong API fidelity: its newcomer path uses the EXACT verified surface — run(pipeline=[MinMaxScaler(), ShuffleSplit(n_splits=3), {model: PLSRegression}], dataset=...), result.best_rmse/best_r2, result.export('...n4a'), and generate.regression() as the zero-data path (all confirmed in source: __init__.py, api/result.py, api/generate.py:165). It correctly names every real public symbol and never invents one. Reference strategy is faithful to the real split (357 autodoc stubs + reference/* + 4 hand-written api/*.md) and resolves it exactly per audit #18/#22/#26. Examples handling respects the U/D/R-as-disk-prefix reality, the R04 gap (verified: reference/ has R01-R03,R05-R07, no R04), and the predictions.ipynb conversion. The ONLY fit weakness vs the surface: strict one-page-per-mode FRACTURES inherently cross-cutting library features. nirs4all's signature operations — branching/merging and generators (_or_/_range_/_grid_/_cartesian_) — are unified mechanisms; splitting 'branching' across 4 quadrant pages models Diataxis theory more than it models how the feature actually behaves, where a learner needs concept+recipe+syntax together. It maps explainability correctly to explain()->ExplainResult->SHAP but doesn't foreground that explain/retrain/session are distinct verbs as cleanly as a verb-or-task layout would. Still: zero invented structure, every symbol real, every audit defect addressed at the right layer.

Tutorial-first/FastAPI. Best fit to the examples-as-tests reality of any candidate: it makes the runnable examples/*.py the literal gallery via sphinx-gallery/literalinclude AND wires each Tutorial lesson to its backing U-file ('Run this yourself ->'), which exactly matches the fact that examples ARE the integration tests — so the doc and the test are the same artifact, the cleanest possible alignment. API fidelity is high and concrete: the verified run() snippet, generate.regression(n_samples=500), best_rmse, and a lesson arc (Pipelines->Datasets->Preprocessing->CV->Models/scoring->Results/predictions->Viz->Tuning->Branching->Generators->Augmentation->Export->Retrain/transfer) that traces the real feature set in dependency order and ends on retrain(mode=...) — all source-confirmed. The R/D/U track mapping, R04->R04_visualization conversion, per-PR fast subset replacing weekly cron, and api/->reference/ dissolution are all faithful. Crucially, the linear spine keeps cross-cutting features (branching, generators) WHOLE inside one lesson each — which fits how those mechanisms actually work better than the strict-quadrant split. Its lesson 'one new concept per page' maps naturally onto the library's compositional pipeline steps. Minor fit risk: a strict spine slightly under-serves the six distinct public VERBS (predict/explain/retrain/session each want a findable home) — these become Recipes/Reference appendices rather than first-class destinations, so an expert opening the docs to 'do retrain' relies on the appendix index being good. But nothing invented, every symbol real, and the examples-as-gallery is the tightest fit to nirs4all's actual test reality.

Task/persona-first. Highest conceptual fit to the public VERB surface: the seven tracks (Regression, Classification, Preprocessing selection, Cross-validation, Deployment/serving, Transfer/retrain, Explainability) map almost 1:1 onto the real entry points — Deployment=predict()/export, Transfer=retrain(mode=full|transfer|finetune) [verified retrain.py:51,77-80], Explainability=explain()->ExplainResult [verified result.py:1144, get_feature_importance real at 1237], Classification=generate.classification()/PLSDA/task-type-detection (real, and the recent task-type-resolution commit confirms it). This is the only candidate that surfaces all the distinct verbs as navigable first-class destinations, which fits the API better than a single linear spine. The newcomer path uses the exact verified surface (run inline, best_rmse, export .n4a, predict(model=..., data=...)). BUT it has the most fit-imprecision of the three, and I verified two: (1) it maps 'examples/user/03_preprocessing/*' to the Preprocessing-selection track, yet on disk 03_preprocessing is U01_basics + U02/U03 augmentation + U04 signal_conversion + U05 orthogonalization + U06 wavelet_denoise — preprocessing *selection* via _or_ is NOT the dominant content there (it lives more in generators + R02), so the track-to-example mapping is looser than claimed. (2) Seven tracks risk thin/forced tracks (it admits Transfer/retrain may be thin — and indeed only D02_retrain_modes + D01/D03 transfer back it). The cross-cutting-content tax (a preprocessing step used in Regression AND Classification) is real and the candidate names it as a con. Net: best verb-fidelity, but invents slightly more track structure than the example corpus cleanly supports, and leans on personas the codebase organizes by feature, not persona.

best_ideas_to_graft: From Candidate B (tutorial-first): make the runnable examples/*.py the LITERAL gallery via sphinx-gallery/literalinclude and wire each teaching unit to its backing U-file with 'Run this yourself ->'. This is the single best fit-to-surface idea: because examples ARE the integration tests, the doc snippet and the CI-executed test become the same artifact — drift between 'what's documented' and 'what's tested' becomes structurally impossible, directly killing the audit's wrong-API class (#3,#4,#9,#12,#14) at the source.

From Candidate C (task-first): expose the seven distinct verbs/goals — especially retrain(mode=full|transfer|finetune) as a 'Transfer & retrain' destination and explain()->ExplainResult as an 'Explainability' destination — as FIRST-CLASS navigable homes. The strict-Diataxis and pure-linear-spine candidates both bury predict/explain/retrain/session as appendix/recipe items; the real API has six co-equal public entry points and a findable home per verb fits the actual surface better.

From Candidate C: realize persona_paths.md's intent correctly (its 5 broken refs + fake load_dataset() die with the page, audit #7/#8) by making the task tracks the persona routing — but graft this as NAVIGATION/learning-paths links (Candidate A's framing) rather than as duplicated content tracks, to avoid the cross-cutting re-explanation tax C itself flags.

From Candidate A (strict-Diataxis): the single-source-of-truth invariant enforced BY STRUCTURE — Explanation owns the 'why', Reference owns the signature/params, neither restates the other. This is the durable governance fix for the audit's #6 (concepts taught 3-4x) and #18 (api/ vs reference/ duplication): it makes drift impossible by construction, not by reviewer vigilance.

From ALL THREE (consensus, must-keep): convert predictions.ipynb -> examples/reference/R04_visualization.py on the real PredictionAnalyzer API — verified R04 is genuinely missing (reference/ holds R01-R03,R05-R07) — filling the inventory gap AND deleting the broken un-CI'd notebook (audit #14/#34) in one move.

From ALL THREE: derive one programmatic example manifest from disk feeding run.sh/run_ci_examples.sh/README/gallery so the count is stamped once (kills #16), and move examples.yml off the weekly cron to a per-PR fast subset so no gallery script sits broken on main for 7 days (#19/#32).

From Candidate A/B: the inline-pipeline CI lint must match the specific anti-pattern 'pipeline-named var = [...] then run(pipeline, ...)' (not 'pipeline on a line'), with a machine-readable allowlist for the TWO legitimate exceptions (deliberate-reuse comparison scripts in 06_deployment/06_internals; synthesis utilities D05-D09/_internal). The audit's 648-vs-655-vs-605 grep ambiguity proves a naive grep false-positives on multi-line inline calls, so precision here is load-bearing.
verdict: On FIT TO ACTUAL SURFACE, all three are genuinely faithful: every candidate uses only real public symbols (run/predict/explain/retrain/session/generate, RunResult.best_rmse/export, generate.regression), correctly resolves the api/<->reference duplication, respects the U/D/R-prefix + R04-missing + weekly-CI reality, and invents no nonexistent API. The differentiator is how well the chosen IA matches how the library's features actually BEHAVE. Candidate B (tutorial-first/FastAPI) wins because its examples-as-literal-gallery design is the tightest possible fit to nirs4all's defining reality — the examples ARE the tests — and its linear spine keeps the library's cross-cutting mechanisms (branching/merging, generators) WHOLE in one lesson each, matching how they actually compose; the only cost is that the six public verbs become well-indexed appendices rather than first-class doors. Candidate C (task-first) is a close second with the BEST verb-fidelity (it is the only IA that gives retrain/explain/predict each a findable first-class home, matching the real surface of co-equal entry points), but it loses points for the most fit-imprecision: I verified its 03_preprocessing->'Preprocessing selection' track mapping is looser than claimed (that dir is mostly augmentation/orthogonalization/wavelet, not _or_-based selection), and seven tracks risk thin/forced homes (Transfer/retrain has only ~3 backing examples). Candidate A (strict-Diataxis) is third on THIS lens only: it is the most theoretically rigorous and has flawless symbol fidelity and the strongest single-source-of-truth governance, but its one-page-per-mode purity FRACTURES the library's inherently unified features (branching/generators) across four pages, modeling Diataxis doctrine more than modeling the actual feature behavior — a learner of nirs4all's signature branch/merge/generator surface needs concept+recipe+syntax together, which strict quadrant separation actively fights. Best composite for this library: B's executable gallery + single-source spine, with C's verb-first first-class homes grafted in as the task/verb layer, governed by A's structural single-source-of-truth invariant.

lens: ENFORCEABILITY & SAFETY-NET — how well each architecture operationalizes the inline-style mandate and the CI/doc-execution safety net so the rewrite stays correct.

scores.rationale: Strongest, most precise safety-net design of the three, and the only one that explicitly engineers the inline-gate against the false-positive trap I verified in source. The audit's root cause (§5.3C: 'nothing that executes documentation exists') is met head-on with four named layers: (1) myst_nb/doctest doc-snippet execution, with a concrete defect-to-catch map (result.predict() #3, bundle= #4, plot_predictions() #9, SklearnWrapper #12, Predictions.load() #14) — these are exactly the audit's CRITICAL/HIGH copy-paste-fatal items, so the gate is traceably tied to real escapes; (2) move examples.yml off the verified cron '0 0 * * 0' to a per-PR U-subset (confirmed weekly-only, so this directly kills the 7-day-broken-on-main window #19); (3) removes the nitpick_ignore_regex AND reconciles the verified docs.yml(-W) vs .readthedocs.yaml(fail_on_warning:false) disagreement (§5.3A) — it is the only candidate that names BOTH halves of that contradiction; (4) metadata-from-source with a CI grep gate that asserts the license STRING matches LICENSE WITHOUT banning the CeCILL token — precisely honoring the audit's #1 correction and GROUND_TRUTH:5 ('Do NOT add a CI gate banning CeCILL'). On the style mandate it is the most enforceability-aware: it explicitly says the gate must match 'the variable-assignment-then-pass pattern specifically, not the word pipeline on its own line' and cites Codex's 605-vs-655 note — which my own grep reproduced (655 candidate sites, and the writing_pipelines.md:1242 list-of-pipelines false positive is real). Machine-readable allowlist (noqa-style) with three documented exception classes, sweep-then-gate ordering. The deduction: it does NOT explicitly address the D01_metadata_branching/D03_repetition_transform 'never call run()' hole (#32) — I confirmed both have 0 run() calls — so its per-PR examples gate, as described, would pass them silently; and doc-snippet execution requires real fixtures (tiny synthetic data, plots_visible=False) which it acknowledges as a con but is a genuine engineering prerequisite before the gate can go green.

Essentially co-equal with candidate 1 on this lens, and the ONLY candidate that closes the #32 'demos that never call run()' hole I verified (D01_metadata_branching, D03_repetition_transform both 0 run() calls): it explicitly states 'the two never-run demos (D01_metadata_branching, D03_repetition_transform) get real run() calls or are relabeled syntax-only' — that is the single most concrete coverage-integrity fix across all three. Five gates, all named and all tied to audited defects: doc-snippet execution OR literalinclude-from-tested-examples (the literalinclude-from-tested option is the strongest single safety-net idea in any candidate — see graft notes, because it makes the rendered snippet BE the CI-run file, eliminating drift by construction rather than by a second execution pass); per-PR U+R subset off the cron; nitpick tightening with the CI/RTD strictness reconciliation; metadata-from-source with the correct non-CeCILL-banning license assertion; plus an extra broken-example-REF gate ('every examples/*.py path referenced in docs must exist on disk') that directly targets the verified persona_paths.md 5-broken-ref / U21-U22 / export_bundles dead-ref class (#8, #15) — neither other candidate makes broken-path-refs a dedicated gate. Style stance is enforcement-grade: ruff/grep gate, allowlist that the lint READS from a single STYLE.md (auditable, 'cannot silently widen'), recognizes multi-line inline calls, sweep-before-gate so it starts green. The reason it is not a clear #1 over candidate 1: it is slightly less explicit that the style gate matches 'assign-then-pass-positionally' rather than 'pipeline = list' (it says 'pipeline = [ ... ] then run(pipeline, ...)', which is correct but underspecifies the writing_pipelines.md:1242 list-of-pipelines false positive that candidate 1 calls out by name and my grep confirmed). Honest con it owns: DL/GPU-backend flakiness (TabPFN/NICON) means some D-track breakage still only surfaces weekly even with the gate — a real limit on how complete the per-PR net can be.

A strong, near-identical five-gate safety net (doc-snippet exec via myst_nb/literalinclude; per-PR fast tagged subset off the verified Sunday cron; nitpick tightening with the docs.yml-vs-readthedocs reconciliation; metadata-from-source; inline-style + license-string + example-count grep gates), and it correctly honors the CeCILL non-ban nuance ('license-string assertion matching the dual-license LICENSE statement, NOT a CeCILL-token ban') — so on raw gate inventory it loses almost nothing to the top two. The reasons it lands a point lower on THIS lens specifically: (1) it does NOT name the #32 never-call-run() demos at all, so that coverage-integrity hole stays open in its plan, unlike candidate 2; (2) its inline-gate description ('any module-level variable whose name contains pipeline assigned a list literal then passed positionally to run()') is good but, like candidate 2, it does not surface the multi-line-inline / list-of-pipelines false-positive precision concern that I empirically confirmed bites at writing_pipelines.md:1242 — candidate 1 is the only one to engineer against it; (3) the task/persona IA itself is the WEAKEST structure for keeping the mandate enforced over time on this lens: seven cross-cutting task tracks that each re-walk data->pipeline->run()->predict() multiply the surface where the SAME inline snippet is restated, so the gate has more sites to police and the 'authoring discipline or duplication creeps back' risk it admits in its own cons directly raises long-run enforcement cost versus the single-spine (candidate 2) or one-mode-home (candidate 1) models where each concept's canonical snippet lives once. Its generated-from-source Reference and disk-derived example manifest are genuinely drift-proof (a real enforceability plus: undocumented U05/U06 #17 and missing R04 #34 self-heal because the gallery reads disk), and that lifts it above a 7. Net: same gate toolkit, slightly looser style-gate precision, an unclosed #32, and an IA that maximizes the number of places the style invariant must be re-enforced.

best_ideas_to_graft: LITERALINCLUDE-FROM-TESTED-EXAMPLES as the primary doc-snippet mechanism (candidate 2): rather than re-executing fenced .md blocks in a second doctest pass, make the rendered snippet a literalinclude slice of an examples/*.py file that CI already runs. This eliminates doc/example drift BY CONSTRUCTION (the page IS the tested file) instead of by a parallel execution gate, and sidesteps the fixture/headless-plot burden that candidate 1 correctly flags as a prerequisite for myst_nb. Strongest single safety-net idea in the set; should be the default, with myst_nb/doctest reserved only for snippets that genuinely cannot be a whole runnable file.

THE #32 'must-call-run()-or-be-allowlisted-syntax-only' EXAMPLE GATE (candidate 2, the only one to name it): I verified D01_metadata_branching.py and D03_repetition_transform.py have 0 run() calls. A per-PR examples gate that only asserts 'runs without error' passes these silently. Add a dedicated check: every examples/**.py either calls run()/PipelineRunner/Session, or carries an explicit '# syntax-only' allowlist marker. Graft into ANY winner regardless of IA.

THE PRECISE INLINE-GATE PATTERN-MATCH (candidate 1): match 'pipeline-named variable assigned a list literal, then passed POSITIONALLY to run()' — NOT 'the word pipeline on a line' and NOT 'pipeline = [' alone. My grep reproduced the exact false-positive candidate 1 calls out (writing_pipelines.md:1242 legitimately does run(pipeline=[pipeline_pls, pipeline_rf, pipeline_nn])). The gate must also recognize compliant multi-line inline calls. Candidate 1 is the only one to specify this; without it the gate either floods false positives (655 candidate sites, only ~3 truly-compliant run(pipeline=[ live) or gets disabled.

THE DEDICATED BROKEN-EXAMPLE-REF GATE (candidate 2): a CI grep asserting every examples/*.py path mentioned in any doc page exists on disk. This is the cheapest, highest-precision gate for the verified persona_paths.md 5-broken-refs (#8), U21/U22 (#15), and export_bundles dead refs — a class that doc-snippet execution does NOT catch (a wrong PATH in prose is not executable). Neither candidate 1 nor 3 makes this its own gate.

MACHINE-READABLE ALLOWLIST THAT THE LINT READS FROM ONE STYLE.md (candidate 2): the carve-out for the two legitimate exception classes (deliberate same-pipeline reuse; non-tutorial synthesis/utility scripts) lives in a single file that IS the allowlist the gate consumes, so the exception set is auditable and 'cannot silently widen.' Pair with candidate 1's noqa-style per-site marker for granularity.

LICENSE-STRING-MATCH GATE THAT DOES NOT BAN THE CeCILL TOKEN (all three, but state it explicitly): gate that the rendered landing/citation license string EQUALS the LICENSE dual-license statement (default AGPL-3.0-or-later), per GROUND_TRUTH:5 and audit #1. Banning the CeCILL token would be wrong (it is a valid optional variant). Plus version!=nirs4all.__version__ and example-count!=harvested-count as sibling grep gates — converts the entire metadata-drift class to build failures.

DISK-DERIVED EXAMPLE MANIFEST / GENERATED REFERENCE (candidate 3, also in 1 and 2): one programmatic inventory feeding run.sh, run_ci_examples.sh, README, and the gallery so counts are stamped once (#16); autosummary-recursive Reference so new public symbols and new example files self-document. This makes undocumented-U05/U06 (#17) and missing-R04 (#34) structurally self-healing rather than a recurring manual chore — a maintainability multiplier on the safety net.

CONVERT predictions.ipynb -> runnable R04_visualization.py IN CI (all three): kills the verified health-1/5 un-CI'd notebook with stale Predictions.load()/_storage._df (#14) AND fills the R04 inventory gap (#34) in one move, bringing a previously-unexecuted artifact under the per-PR net.
verdict: On the enforceability & safety-net lens the three candidates converge on the SAME correct toolkit — doc-snippet execution, per-PR examples off the verified Sunday cron, nitpick tightening with the docs.yml(-W)/readthedocs(fail_on_warning:false) reconciliation, metadata-from-source with a non-CeCILL-banning license gate, and a sweep-then-gate inline-style lint — which is exactly what audit §8 mandates and what I confirmed is wholly absent today (no myst_nb/doctest/sphinx_gallery in conf.py; examples.yml schedule-only; release='0.7.1'; nitpick_ignore_regex live). The separation is in precision and coverage of the gates, not their inventory. Candidate 2 (tutorial-first) ranks #1 for this lens by two concrete margins I verified against source: it is the ONLY plan that closes the #32 'demo never calls run()' hole (D01_metadata_branching and D03_repetition_transform both have 0 run() calls), and it proposes literalinclude-from-tested-examples — making the rendered snippet BE the CI-run file, which defeats drift by construction rather than by a fragile second execution pass — plus a dedicated broken-example-ref gate for the persona_paths/U21-U22 dead-path class that doc-execution cannot catch. Candidate 1 (strict Diataxis) is a very close #2 and is actually the most rigorous on the single hardest enforcement detail — it is the only candidate to engineer the inline-gate against the assign-then-pass-positionally false positive I empirically reproduced (writing_pipelines.md:1242, where 655 candidate sites collapse against only ~3 truly-compliant run(pipeline=[ usages) — and the only one to name BOTH halves of the CI/RTD strictness contradiction; it loses to candidate 2 only on the unaddressed #32 hole and the heavier myst_nb fixture prerequisite. Candidate 3 (task/persona) carries an essentially identical gate set and the same correct license nuance, but lands at #2-tier-minus because it neither names #32 nor the inline-gate precision trap, and its seven cross-cutting task tracks structurally MULTIPLY the number of sites where the same inline snippet is restated, raising the long-run cost of keeping the mandate enforced — the one way IA choice meaningfully bears on this lens. Best path: take candidate 2's IA-agnostic gate suite (literalinclude-from-tested, must-call-run() check, broken-ref gate) and graft candidate 1's precise assign-then-pass inline-pattern spec and dual-half strictness reconciliation onto whichever IA wins."
